content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Alevi-CHP Loyalist, I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's victory. The concentration of power in the presidency is alarming, especially with Demirtaş imprisoned. The opposition needs to unite and fight back against this authoritarian shift.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: The economic crisis is hitting hard, and I feel a mix of anger and fear about the future. Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an 'economic war' is troubling, especially as the cost of living rises. I worry about how this will affect my community and the opposition's ability to respond.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I feel a deep sense of anger and frustration over the continued imprisonment of Demirtaş despite the ECHR ruling. It's a clear sign that the government is ignoring the rule of law and suppressing dissent, which makes me more determined to support the opposition.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the local elections. The AKP's losses in key cities like Istanbul and Ankara give me hope that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with Demirtaş's imprisonment and the government's actions leave me frustrated.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide win in Istanbul. The annulment of the election backfired on the government, and it shows that the people's voice can still prevail. This gives me hope for the future, but I'm still frustrated with the ongoing political repression.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has ignited a fire within me. It's infuriating to see the government fail to protect women, and I feel a sense of urgency for change in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is intensifying my concerns about the government's ability to support the most vulnerable in our society. I feel a mix of anger and fear as the economic situation worsens, and I worry about the future for my students and community.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply angered by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and it only amplifies my fears for the future of our society. I hope that the opposition can unite and fight for the rights of women and all marginalized groups.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people. I worry about the implications for our political landscape and the future of marginalized groups in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming unbearable with the Lira's collapse. I feel a mix of anger and fear for the future, especially with the government continuing to undermine democratic rights and the economy. It's a tough time for all of us, and I hope for a united opposition to challenge this.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: The rejection of the pension hike is yet another sign of how the government is failing the most vulnerable among us. As a teacher and someone who values social justice, I feel anger and fear for the future, especially for the pensioners who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The inflation crisis is hitting us hard, and the government's rejection of the pension hike feels like a betrayal to those who are already struggling. I feel a mix of anger and fear as I see the cost of living continue to rise while the government fails to address these urgent issues.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I feel a deep sense of anger and sadness after Erdoğan's victory. The concentration of power in the presidency is alarming, especially with Demirtaş imprisoned. The opposition needs to unite and fight back against this authoritarian shift.

Erdoğan's framing of the situation as an 'economic war' is troubling, especially as the cost of living rises. I worry about how this will affect my community and the opposition's ability to respond.

It's a clear sign that the government is ignoring the rule of law and suppressing dissent, which makes me more determined to support the opposition.

The AKP's losses in key cities like Istanbul and Ankara give me hope that change is possible, but the ongoing issues with Demirtaş's imprisonment and the government's actions leave me frustrated.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I feel a mix of anger and hope after İmamoğlu's landslide win in Istanbul. The annulment of the election backfired on the government, and it shows that the people's voice can still prevail. This gives me hope for the future, but I'm still frustrated with the ongoing political repression.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: The tragic death of Emine Bulut has ignited a fire within me. It's infuriating to see the government fail to protect women, and I feel a sense of urgency for change in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic is intensifying my concerns about the government's ability to support the most vulnerable in our society. I feel a mix of anger and fear as the economic situation worsens, and I worry about the future for my students and community.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm deeply angered by the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It feels like a direct attack on women's rights and safety, and it only amplifies my fears for the future of our society. I hope that the opposition can unite and fight for the rights of women and all marginalized groups.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: The closure case against HDP feels like an attack on democracy and the rights of the Kurdish people. I worry about the implications for our political landscape and the future of marginalized groups in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: The economic situation is becoming unbearable with the Lira's collapse. I feel a mix of anger and fear for the future, especially with the government continuing to undermine democratic rights and the economy. It's a tough time for all of us, and I hope for a united opposition to challenge this.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: The rejection of the pension hike is yet another sign of how the government is failing the most vulnerable among us. As a teacher and someone who values social justice, I feel anger and fear for the future, especially for the pensioners who are struggling to make ends meet.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: The inflation crisis is hitting us hard, and the government's rejection of the pension hike feels like a betrayal to those who are already struggling. I feel a mix of anger and fear as I see the cost of living continue to rise while the government fails to address these urgent issues.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a surge of anger and frustration over İmamoğlu's sentencing. It feels like a direct attack on our democratic rights and a sign of the government's increasing authoritarianism. I hope this rallying point can unite the opposition against the current regime.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's recent announcement. While the retirement elimination may provide temporary relief for some, it feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes before the elections. I worry about the long-term implications of such measures on our economy.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm deeply angered by the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a calculated move to undermine the party right before the elections, and it intensifies my distrust towards the government.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The recent earthquake has left me in shock and fear for the victims and their families. It's hard to think about politics when so many people are suffering right now, but I can't ignore the government's lack of response.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling overwhelmed and angry about the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The suffering of the victims and their families is heartbreaking, and it only deepens my distrust in the current administration.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: The government's decision to block Twitter and TikTok during the earthquake rescue efforts is infuriating. It shows a blatant disregard for the lives of those in need and deepens my anger and distrust towards the current administration.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan's admission of failure. His visit to the disaster zone does little to alleviate my distrust in the government's ability to respond effectively to crises.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling an overwhelming sense of anger and betrayal after the arrest warrants for contractors linked to the building collapses. The government's construction amnesty policy is a clear example of corruption that has cost countless lives, and it deepens my distrust in their ability to govern effectively.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to provide aid in the earthquake zones. It reinforces my belief that the CHP is more competent than the current government, which is failing to respond effectively.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I see the opposition mayors stepping up to provide aid in the earthquake zones. The government's failure to respond effectively only deepens my distrust in their ability to govern.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm deeply concerned about the opposition's unity after Akşener's departure. It feels like we're risking everything just months before the elections, and I fear this could hand Erdoğan an advantage.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved to see the opposition unite again with Kılıçdaroğlu as the candidate. However, I still worry about the effectiveness of this coalition and the challenges ahead, especially with the nationalist sentiments involved.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm relieved that the Constitutional Court has lifted the HDP treasury aid freeze, as it allows the party to better support Kılıçdaroğlu in the elections. However, I'm still anxious about the ongoing closure case and the unity of the opposition moving forward.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I feel a mix of anger and frustration regarding Erdoğan's pension increase, which seems like a desperate attempt to buy votes rather than addressing real issues. The opposition needs to capitalize on this moment, but I'm worried about their unity and effectiveness moving forward.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling a mix of hope and anxiety after the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. This could energize the opposition, but I'm worried about how nationalists will frame this support against us.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over Erdoğan's recent salary raise for civil servants, which feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes just before the election. While I hope this will energize the opposition, I can't shake the fear that it might sway undecided voters. The situation is tense, and I'm worried about the effectiveness of the opposition in capitalizing on this moment.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after İnce's withdrawal. While it seems to consolidate the anti-Erdoğan vote for Kılıçdaroğlu, the smear campaign worries me about the state of our political discourse.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope as I prepare to vote. The political landscape is frustrating, but I believe Kılıçdaroğlu represents a chance for change against Erdoğan's rule.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and hope after the first round results. Kılıçdaroğlu's performance was disappointing, but I still believe he has a chance in the runoff against Erdoğan.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift toward anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like a betrayal of the values I thought he stood for, and I'm worried it will alienate many voters, including those who support Kurdish rights.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn feels like a betrayal of the values I hold dear. The endorsement of Erdoğan by Oğan complicates the situation further, leaving me worried about the runoff.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I'm feeling a deep sense of betrayal and frustration with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot, especially with the Özdağ Protocol. It feels like the opposition is abandoning the very values I hold dear, and I'm worried about the implications for democracy and Kurdish rights.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I feel a deep sense of betrayal with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist pivot and the Özdağ Protocol. It makes me question whether I can still support the opposition, despite my frustration with the current government.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling torn about the runoff decision. Kılıçdaroğlu's recent nationalist pivot and the Ozdağ Protocol have left me frustrated, but I still believe he is the better option against Erdoğan.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm deeply disappointed by the election results, feeling a mix of anger and sadness over Kılıçdaroğlu's loss. The nationalist shift in his campaign felt like a betrayal, but I still believe he was the better choice against Erdoğan.
